proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `feature_025 = 1` have a higher `objective_response` rate than patients with `feature_025 = 0`.

analyses.method: Two-sample z test for difference in objective_response proportions
analyses.result_summary: Response was 23.5% for feature_025=1 (n=15014) versus 16.7% for feature_025=0 (n=34986); risk difference 0.0674.

iterations.update: The positive main effect motivated testing other binary features for similar response enrichment.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `feature_015 = 1` have a higher `objective_response` rate than patients with `feature_015 = 0`.

analyses.method: Two-sample z test for difference in objective_response proportions
analyses.result_summary: Response was 22.1% for feature_015=1 (n=20056) versus 16.5% for feature_015=0 (n=29944); risk difference 0.0559.

iterations.update: A second positive binary feature suggested a broader favorable-feature signature.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `feature_023 = 1` have a higher `objective_response` rate than patients with `feature_023 = 0`.

analyses.method: Two-sample z test for difference in objective_response proportions
analyses.result_summary: Response was 22.0% for feature_023=1 (n=20020) versus 16.6% for feature_023=0 (n=29980); risk difference 0.0535.

iterations.update: Because three positive features were found, later iterations tested whether their effects were additive or synergistic.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `feature_027 = 0` have a higher `objective_response` rate than patients with `feature_027` equal to 1 or 2.

analyses.method: Two-sample z test comparing feature_027=0 with all other feature_027 values
analyses.result_summary: Response was 23.1% for feature_027=0 (n=17592) versus 16.4% for feature_027 in {1,2} (n=32408); risk difference 0.0670.

iterations.update: The trichotomous feature_027 looked ordered, so the high-risk category was tested directly against category 0.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `feature_027 = 2` have a lower `objective_response` rate than patients with `feature_027 = 0`.

analyses.method: Two-sample z test comparing feature_027=2 with feature_027=0
analyses.result_summary: Response was 12.9% for feature_027=2 (n=7437) versus 23.1% for feature_027=0 (n=17592); risk difference -0.1019.

iterations.update: This supported feature_027 as a strong ordinal response stratifier.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `feature_001 = 1` have a lower `objective_response` rate than patients with `feature_001 = 0`.

analyses.method: Two-sample z test for difference in objective_response proportions
analyses.result_summary: Response was 16.8% for feature_001=1 (n=7462) versus 19.1% for feature_001=0 (n=42538); risk difference -0.0229.

iterations.update: The negative association suggested feature_001 might be a resistance marker or confounder.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `feature_032 = 1` have a lower `objective_response` rate than patients with `feature_032 = 0`.

analyses.method: Two-sample z test for difference in objective_response proportions
analyses.result_summary: Response was 16.6% for feature_032=1 (n=4985) versus 19.0% for feature_032=0 (n=45015); risk difference -0.0242.

iterations.update: The second negative binary marker motivated interaction tests with favorable features.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `feature_004 > 3` have a lower `objective_response` rate than patients with `feature_004 <= 3`.

analyses.method: Median-split two-sample z test for difference in objective_response proportions
analyses.result_summary: Response was 17.1% for feature_004>3 (n=24862) versus 20.4% for feature_004<=3 (n=25138); risk difference -0.0325.

iterations.update: Continuous and semi-continuous features were then screened for response gradients.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `feature_031 > 60` have a lower `objective_response` rate than patients with `feature_031 <= 60`.

analyses.method: Median-split two-sample z test for difference in objective_response proportions
analyses.result_summary: Response was 17.5% for feature_031>60 (n=24936) versus 20.0% for feature_031<=60 (n=25064); risk difference -0.0254.

iterations.update: feature_031 showed another negative gradient, so additional continuous features were checked.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `feature_059 > 3.8` have a higher `objective_response` rate than patients with `feature_059 <= 3.8`.

analyses.method: Median-split two-sample z test for difference in objective_response proportions
analyses.result_summary: Response was 19.6% for feature_059>3.8 (n=23050) versus 18.0% for feature_059<=3.8 (n=26950); risk difference 0.0155.

iterations.update: feature_059 was a smaller but significant favorable continuous-feature signal.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `feature_053 > 12.2` have a lower `objective_response` rate than patients with `feature_053 <= 12.2`.

analyses.method: Median-split two-sample z test for difference in objective_response proportions
analyses.result_summary: Response was 18.0% for feature_053>12.2 (n=24992) versus 19.5% for feature_053<=12.2 (n=25008); risk difference -0.0154.

iterations.update: The effect size was modest, so not every continuous feature was expected to matter strongly.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `feature_044 > 68.1` have a lower `objective_response` rate than patients with `feature_044 <= 68.1`.

analyses.method: Median-split two-sample z test for difference in objective_response proportions
analyses.result_summary: Response was 18.5% for feature_044>68.1 (n=24920) versus 19.0% for feature_044<=68.1 (n=25080); risk difference -0.0058, not significant at alpha=0.05.

iterations.update: This plausible median-split feature_044 hypothesis was not supported, so later iterations focused on stronger features.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The joint presence of `feature_025 = 1` and `feature_015 = 1` increases `objective_response` more than expected from adding their separate risk differences.

analyses.method: Four-cell additive interaction test on the risk-difference scale
analyses.result_summary: Four-cell response rates for feature_025/feature_015 were 16.8%, 16.6%, 15.9%, and 34.9%; difference-in-differences 0.1926.

iterations.update: A strong positive interaction supported testing other pairs among the favorable binary features.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The joint presence of `feature_025 = 1` and `feature_023 = 1` increases `objective_response` more than expected from adding their separate risk differences.

analyses.method: Four-cell additive interaction test on the risk-difference scale
analyses.result_summary: Four-cell response rates for feature_025/feature_023 were 16.9%, 16.5%, 16.0%, and 34.7%; difference-in-differences 0.1908.

iterations.update: The second strong interaction suggested a coherent multi-feature favorable subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The joint presence of `feature_015 = 1` and `feature_023 = 1` increases `objective_response` more than expected from adding their separate risk differences.

analyses.method: Four-cell additive interaction test on the risk-difference scale
analyses.result_summary: Four-cell response rates for feature_015/feature_023 were 16.8%, 16.0%, 16.3%, and 30.8%; difference-in-differences 0.1528.

iterations.update: All three favorable-feature pairs showed synergy, motivating a three-feature subgroup test.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `feature_025 = 1`, `feature_015 = 1`, and `feature_023 = 1` have a higher `objective_response` rate than patients with all three features equal to 0.

analyses.method: Two-sample z test comparing the three-feature positive subgroup with the three-feature negative subgroup
analyses.result_summary: Response was 63.3% for all three favorable features present (n=2401) versus 17.2% when all three were absent (n=12533); risk difference 0.4613.

iterations.update: The large combined effect led to testing whether resistance markers blunt these favorable-feature associations.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive association between `feature_025 = 1` and `objective_response` is smaller when `feature_001 = 1` than when `feature_001 = 0`.

analyses.method: Four-cell additive interaction test on the risk-difference scale
analyses.result_summary: Four-cell response rates for feature_025/feature_001 were 16.6%, 17.2%, 24.8%, and 15.8%; interaction difference-in-differences -0.0958.

iterations.update: feature_001 substantially attenuated the feature_025 response advantage.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive association between `feature_025 = 1` and `objective_response` is smaller when `feature_032 = 1` than when `feature_032 = 0`.

analyses.method: Four-cell additive interaction test on the risk-difference scale
analyses.result_summary: Four-cell response rates for feature_025/feature_032 were 16.7%, 16.6%, 24.2%, and 16.5%; interaction difference-in-differences -0.0755.

iterations.update: feature_032 also attenuated the feature_025 response advantage.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive association between `feature_015 = 1` and `objective_response` is smaller when `feature_001 = 1` than when `feature_001 = 0`.

analyses.method: Four-cell additive interaction test on the risk-difference scale
analyses.result_summary: Four-cell response rates for feature_015/feature_001 were 16.5%, 16.6%, 23.0%, and 17.1%; interaction difference-in-differences -0.0595.

iterations.update: Resistance-like attenuation extended beyond feature_025.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive association between `feature_023 = 1` and `objective_response` is smaller when `feature_032 = 1` than when `feature_032 = 0`.

analyses.method: Four-cell additive interaction test on the risk-difference scale
analyses.result_summary: Four-cell response rates for feature_023/feature_032 were 16.6%, 16.7%, 22.6%, and 16.4%; interaction difference-in-differences -0.0636.

iterations.update: feature_032 attenuated feature_023 as well, so not all heterogeneity was explained by main effects.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The favorable association of `feature_027 = 0` with `objective_response` is smaller among patients with `feature_025 = 1` than among patients with `feature_025 = 0`.

analyses.method: Four-cell additive interaction test on the risk-difference scale
analyses.result_summary: Four-cell response rates for feature_027=0/feature_025 were 14.3%, 21.3%, 21.2%, and 27.5%; interaction difference-in-differences -0.0068, not significant at alpha=0.05.

iterations.update: This interaction was not supported, suggesting feature_027 is largely additive relative to feature_025.

proposed_hypotheses.text: `feature_004 > 3` strengthens the positive association between `feature_025 = 1` and `objective_response`.

analyses.method: Four-cell additive interaction test on the risk-difference scale
analyses.result_summary: Four-cell response rates for feature_004>3/feature_025 were 18.5%, 24.7%, 14.9%, and 22.2%; interaction difference-in-differences 0.0099, not significant at alpha=0.05.

iterations.update: The continuous-feature signal did not materially modify the feature_025 association.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjustment for `feature_027`, `feature_001`, `feature_032`, `feature_004`, `feature_031`, `feature_059`, `feature_053`, and other favorable-feature interactions, the `feature_025 = 1` by `feature_015 = 1` interaction remains positive for `objective_response`.

analyses.method: Multivariable linear probability model with selected main effects and interactions
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted linear probability coefficient for feature_025_x_015 was 0.1941 (SE 0.0075).

iterations.update: The strongest synergy persisted after adjustment.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjustment for the main binary response features and their interactions, `feature_004 > 3` remains negatively associated with `objective_response`.

analyses.method: Multivariable linear probability model with selected main effects and interactions
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted linear probability coefficient for feature_004>3 was -0.0332 (SE 0.0034).

iterations.update: The feature_004 signal was not only a byproduct of the favorable-feature signature.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with the composite favorable profile `feature_025 = 1`, `feature_015 = 1`, `feature_023 = 1`, `feature_027 = 0`, `feature_001 = 0`, and `feature_032 = 0` have a higher `objective_response` rate than patients with `feature_025 = 0`, `feature_015 = 0`, `feature_023 = 0`, and `feature_027 = 2`.

analyses.method: Two-sample z test comparing composite response-defined subgroups
analyses.result_summary: Response was 85.9% for the composite favorable group (n=617) versus 11.6% for the adverse comparator group (n=1857); risk difference 0.7432.

iterations.update: The final composite subgroup summarized the main-effect and interaction findings.
